target.name: Sila Nanotechnologies
target.founder: Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, Gene Berdichevsky

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sila Nanotechnologies, a late-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Gleb Yushin, Alex Jacobs, and Gene Berdichevsky, is valued at $3.3B post-money following $375M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (Livent, ticker LNZA) and 8 private firms including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape—all operating in adjacent energy and advanced materials segments.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.2x multiplier typical for late-stage advancement, yields $4.0B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—TAE Technologies at $6.0B (Series G), Form Energy at $3.5B (Series G), and Ascend Elements at $3.0B (Growth)—produces a median of $3.5B, with a range from $3.0B to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.0B to $4.6B, with a median of $3.7B. The current $3.3B valuation sits near the bottom quartile of this range. Key uncertainties include technology readiness and commercialization timeline for silicon-based anode solutions, commodity price volatility in battery materials, and relative competitive positioning within the crowded advanced battery landscape. The peer set exhibits significant valuation dispersion—a 2.0x spread—reflecting stage and commercialization risk heterogeneity.
